name: Micromedex Drug Information API
what_it_does: Micromedex (Merative) REST API for clinical drug information and toxicology data platform. Enables AI agents to access comprehensive drug monograph data including dosing, mechanism, pharmacokinetics, and adverse effects for clinical decision support, retrieve drug interaction data with clinical significance ratings for medication safety checking, access DRUGDEX evidence-based drug evaluations for clinical decision making, retrieve POISINDEX and TOMES toxicology data for poison control and emergency medicine, access drug identification and imprint lookup for medication reconciliation, handle disease and condition-specific drug therapy recommendations, retrieve IV compatibility and admixture data for pharmacy, access neonatal and pediatric dosing data from NeoFax, retrieve alternative medicine and natural product data, and integrate clinical drug intelligence with EHR, pharmacy, and CDS platforms.
use_cases: Screening drug interactions from clinical decision support agents
Retrieving toxicology data from poison control automation agents
Accessing evidence-based drug evaluations from clinical agents
Checking IV compatibility from pharmacy workflow agents
not_for: Consumer drug information without clinical accuracy requirements
Administrative pharmacy (NDC pricing) without clinical content
Non-medical applications without licensed clinical drug data need
best_when: A health system, hospital pharmacy, or clinical software vendor using Micromedex wants AI agents to automate drug interaction screening, toxicology lookups, evidence-based therapy recommendations, and IV compatibility checking.
avoid_when: PATIENT SAFETY RISK: Micromedex drug interaction and dosing data supports clinical decisions — automated medication order approval based solely on API results without pharmacist or prescriber verification violates clinical safety standards. POISINDEX toxicology data requires poison control specialist interpretation. DRUGDEX evidence ratings require clinical context. Automated therapy recommendations without prescriber review creates patient safety liability.

auth.notes: Micromedex uses API key and OAuth 2.0 authentication. Per-institution licensing. Originally Truven Health Analytics → acquired by IBM Watson Health (2016) → divested to Francisco Partners → rebranded as Merative (2022). Merative is standalone health data company. No webhooks — static drug reference queries. REST API with JSON. Chicago, Illinois (Merative HQ). Competes with Lexicomp (Wolters Kluwer) and First Databank for clinical drug database market.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual institutional license
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual license by institution type, user count, and content modules
pricing.notes: Chicago, Illinois. Founded 1969. IBM Watson Health → Merative (2022). DRUGDEX, POISINDEX, NeoFax, TOMES, and Micromedex branded content. Strong in poison control (POISINDEX used by US poison control centers). NeoFax for neonatal dosing. Used by 4,700+ hospitals globally. Competes with Lexicomp (Wolters Kluwer) and First Databank (Hearst) for hospital CDS.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; institution and module dependent

known_agent_gotchas: PATIENT SAFETY RISK: DRUGDEX evidence ratings are for clinical decision support, not automation; automated therapy decisions without clinician review create patient safety liability
Merative rebrand (2022) — IBM Watson Health became Merative; API documentation, support contacts, and branding changed; verify current Merative documentation
Content module complexity — DRUGDEX, POISINDEX, NeoFax, TOMES, and drug interactions are separate modules; API access requires appropriate licensed module
POISINDEX poison control use — POISINDEX data is used by US poison control centers; automated toxicology responses without poison control specialist consultation are dangerous
Drug database update cadence — Micromedex updates vary by module; some content is real-time, some is quarterly; understand update frequency for safety-critical data
NeoFax neonatal dosing — neonatal dosing is weight-, age- and gestational-age-dependent; validate all calculation logic independently of raw NeoFax data
agent_readiness.security_notes: Clinical drug information. SOC2, HIPAA, ISO27001. API key/OAuth2. US/EU. Patient medication and toxicology data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Clinical drug data and toxicology. SOC2, HIPAA. API key/OAuth2. US. Patient medication and poison control data.
